htmlContent:
Slide 1

NCDOT & NCDIT – T

Florence Response Utilizing GIS

“Get as much verified information as possible, to as many officials as possible, as soon as possible”

November 7, 2018 John Farley NCDOT GIS Manager

Slide 2

Times They Are a Changin’

Hurricane Florence

  • 2400 road closures during Florence
  • 1600 max at one time
Road Closures by Road Type
Road Type#%
Interstate221%23%
US2069%
NC33414%
Secondary186077%77%
Total2422100%

Slide 4

Hurricane Florence Disaster Event Time Line

Business Impacts Events IT Actions Legend: Red = Hurricane Florence events Orange = DMV impacts Green = GIS actions Blue = Web Services actions Yellow = Infrastructure actions Activate alternate DMV Cust Contact Ctr STARS Help Desk Closed Emergency Msg to IVR LPAs Resume normal duties 63 DMV LPAs closed Drone Imagery Flown Drone Imagery Published Mon 9/10 Tues 9/11 Wed 9/12 Thurs 9/13 Fri 9/14 Sat 9/15 Sun 9/16 Mon 9/17 Tues 9/18 Wed 9/19 Communicate w all DOT Bus Units to ID BEFs TIMS support assigned in DOT Traffic Mgmt Call Ctrs Alternate IT Help Desk Ready TIMS History Event Map started 0365 hurricane collaboration sites stood up Web Team staff available 24x7 IT Recommend s DMV close Bladen DMV Closed Start Rcvg Emergency data requests GIS Servers stressed thru Oct 11 Drone Imagery published GIS Coordination begins Hydraulics & Facilities Mapping Hotel Mapping Drone Mapping AGOL Shelter Map Div 3 Maps for Secratary Daily TIMS Maps Sat Sept 1 – Sun 30 Inundated w new AGOL/123 Accounts Req (460 total) Fuel Depots Data Requests Wed Oct 3 Lenoir impact for Kinston Sun Sept 23 MOA Maps

Slide 5

UAS – Unmanned Aerial Systems

  • NCDOT deployed 14 teams
  • Over 3200 Images and Videos were collected

Slide 6

Image Capture

Slide 7

Video Capture

US 117

Slide 8

Storm Maps

Slide 9

Hurricane Florence Facilities Status Map (“The Kitchen Sink”)

Slide 10

GO!NC

NCDOT Weather Event Information Map

~ NCDOT Weather Event Information Map - Created 9/11 - 26,270 views (this is our original TIMS and weather data map and is probably more public oriented) Most of the views were generated in that time window with over 200 times increase of daily viewing from about 15 to over 3000.

Slide 11

Storm Apps

  • Drone Map and Display
  • Secretary’s Mobile Drone Mission Assignment
  • Pipe Inventory, Assessment, and Replacement (mobile component)
  • Traffic Information Management System (TIMS) GIS Integration
  • Mobile Damage Assessment
  • Mobile Debris Removal

Slide 12

Florence After Action and Lessons Learned

  • Finishing the After Action Report for DIT – T
    • Majority are GIS and/or Data Management Related
  • Meeting with the business to consolidate and prioritize recommendations

Slide 13

Florence Some Recommendations…

  • Daily Geospatial Call – Patterned on the one for FEMA
  • State GIS Staff (Resources?) for local assistance
  • Etc…

Slide 14

Questions…

Slide 15

Let’s Connect!

@NCDIT @BroadbandIO @ncicenter

@NCDIT

NC DIT
